I will only feel comfortable in shared cursor spaces once I am given more control over the general appearance of the cursor, if it is meant to embody me as a person
when working with colleagues in a shared cursor space, try to give them 5-10 pixels of space, considered one's "personal cursor space bubble"
when joining a shared cursor space, it is polite to touch the tips of your cursors with all the other guests, in order of social hierarchy
I grappled with this for the first time on some client work a few weeks ago, I'm sure I am not alone https://t.co/LV3SxlJ4Uh
someone is gonna need to write a social etiquette guide to shared-cursor spaces, I have enough anxiety as it is
